title: carex serotina subsp. fennica
intro: Carex serotina subsp. fennica, also known as Fenn's sedge, is a subspecies of Carex serotina, a type of sedgy grass that is native to the northern hemisphere. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Carex serotina subsp. fennica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried leaves and stems can be used in herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
